title: Sales Support Coordinator
description:
The Sales Support Coordinator position is a supporting role, keeping prospect and client data current in the CRM, enriching account information, and conducting account/prospect research. Provide administrative assistance to sales teams to make them more productive. Completes administrative duties for the sales team like overseeing daily assignments for the team, may handle escalations, involve in following up activities.

About The Role

In this opportunity as a Sales Support Coordinator, you will


Update prospect/client information in CRM
Obtain and provide prospects/client information regarding market intelligence and penetration
Update information in salesforce.com and EMS
Support the sales team with data enrichment & account / prospect research
Data analysis of closed deals from SDR team
Supporting sales lead mapping & process execution


About You

You’re a fit for the role of Sales Support Coordinator if your background includes:


Entry level professional roles requiring a discipline specific degree or the equivalent work experience that provides knowledge and exposure to fundamental theories, principles, and concepts
General professional and conceptual knowledge of theories and principles within a discipline
Basic understanding of the roles of relevant functional groups within own area
Applies general knowledge of business, developed through education, to make informed judgements
Degree-level education is required.
Sets priorities to meet deadlines; develops plans to meet short-term objectives
Applies a wide range of policies or standards in straightforward situations
Decisions involve interpretation of data, planning own work, or refining the methods and techniques to be used
Uses general professional or specialized knowledge to solve routine problems
Decisions are guided by policies, procedures and business acumen, impacting the efficiency and effectiveness of own area
Shift Time: 2PM to 11PM IST


What’s in it For You?


Hybrid Work Model: We’ve adopted a flexible hybrid working environment (2-3 days a week in the office depending on the role) for our office-based roles while delivering a seamless experience that is digitally and physically connected.
Flexibility & Work-Life Balance: Flex My Way is a set of supportive workplace policies designed to help manage personal and professional responsibilities, whether caring for family, giving back to the community, or finding time to refresh and reset. This builds upon our flexible work arrangements, including work from anywhere for up to 8 weeks per year, empowering employees to achieve a better work-life balance.
Career Development and Growth: By fostering a culture of continuous learning and skill development, we prepare our talent to tackle tomorrow’s challenges and deliver real-world solutions. Our Grow My Way programming and skills-first approach ensures you have the tools and knowledge to grow, lead, and thrive in an AI-enabled future.
Industry Competitive Benefits: We offer comprehensive benefit plans to include flexible vacation, two company-wide Mental Health Days off, access to the Headspace app, retirement savings, tuition reimbursement, employee incentive programs, and resources for mental, physical, and financial wellbeing.
Culture: Globally recognized, award-winning reputation for inclusion and belonging, flexibility, work-life balance, and more. We live by our values: Obsess over our Customers, Compete to Win, Challenge (Y)our Thinking, Act Fast / Learn Fast, and Stronger Together.
Social Impact: Make an impact in your community with our Social Impact Institute. We offer employees two paid volunteer days off annually and opportunities to get involved with pro-bono consulting projects and Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) initiatives.
Making a Real-World Impact: We are one of the few companies globally that helps its customers pursue justice, truth, and transparency. Together, with the professionals and institutions we serve, we help uphold the rule of law, turn the wheels of commerce, catch bad actors, report the facts, and provide trusted, unbiased information to people all over the world.


About Us

Thomson Reuters informs the way forward by bringing together the trusted content and technology that people and organizations need to make the right decisions. We serve professionals across legal, tax, accounting, compliance, government, and media. Our products combine highly specialized software and insights to empower professionals with the data, intelligence, and solutions needed to make informed decisions, and to help institutions in their pursuit of justice, truth, and transparency. Reuters, part of Thomson Reuters, is a world leading provider of trusted journalism and news.

We are powered by the talents of 26,000 employees across more than 70 countries, where everyone has a chance to contribute and grow professionally in flexible work environments. At a time when objectivity, accuracy, fairness, and transparency are under attack, we consider it our duty to pursue them. Sound exciting? Join us and help shape the industries that move society forward.

As a global business, we rely on the unique backgrounds, perspectives, and experiences of all employees to deliver on our business goals. To ensure we can do that, we seek talented, qualified employees in all our operations around the world regardless of race, color, sex/gender, including pregnancy, gender identity and expression, national origin, religion, sexual orientation, disability, age, marital status, citizen status, veteran status, or any other protected classification under applicable law. Thomson Reuters is proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity Employer providing a drug-free workplace.

We also make reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities and for sincerely held religious beliefs in accordance with applicable law. More information on requesting an accommodation here.

Learn more on how to protect yourself from fraudulent job postings here.

More information about Thomson Reuters can be found on thomsonreuters.com.

title: Associate Data Scientist (BI - Reporting)
description:
Level: Associate (L3)

Role: BI Reporting – MSO Ops

Mio Overview

T The Marketing Science Operations (MSO) vertical at Amgen is the operational backbone that ensures accurate, timely, governed, and analytics ready marketing data across all digital channels. MSO enables the MIO (Marketing Investment & Optimization), and broader Decision Sciences teams to run self-service analytics, generate insights, and execute campaigns reliably.

MSO focuses on data readiness, governance, pipeline operations, vendor accountability, and automation with the goal of delivering clean, standardized, and insight-ready datasets.

Role Summary

The Associate – MSO BI Reporting role is entry level analytics and reporting position within the Marketing Science Operations (MSO) BI team. This role is ideal for candidates who are interested in building strong foundations in business intelligence, reporting, and data quality.

The team will provide structured learning and on‑the‑job exposure. What matters most is curiosity, critical thinking, and a strong learning mindset.

LIVE | WHAT YOU WILL DO

As an MSO BI Reporting Associate, you will work under the guidance of Senior Associates and Managers to support BI reporting, dashboarding, and data quality operations.

You will:


Support development and maintenance of standard BI dashboards and reports.
Perform data validation, reconciliation, and QA checks to ensure accuracy and consistency.
Assist in data extraction, transformation, and preparation for reporting use cases.
Monitor data refreshes, pipeline health, and reporting completeness.
Help document metric definitions, data sources, and reporting logic.
Support regular weekly and monthly reporting cycles.
Identify data issues, anomalies, or gaps and escalate appropriately; Closely collaborate with Data Engineering stakeholders.


Basic Qualifications


Bachelor's (3 to 6 Yrs) or master's (2 to 4 Yrs) degree required in any discipline with strong record of academic success in quantitative and analytic coursework such as operations research, applied mathematics, management science, data science, statistics, econometrics, or engineering.
Experience in Tableau, Power BI, or similar visualization tools; Familiarity with dashboards, KPIs, or recurring reports
Effective oral and written communication skills.
Comfort working with structured data, tables, and reports.


Preferred Qualifications


Basic experience with Excel, SQL, or any BI/reporting tool
Interest in data governance, reporting standards, or analytics operations.
Experience supporting analytics in pharma, healthcare, or regulated environments.
Effective communication skills with the ability to collaborate closely with Senior Associates to deliver high‑quality analytical outputs.
Exposure to DevSecOps practice
Exposure to Incident Management

Alert relevant stakeholders about delays, disruptions, or potential issues
Collaborate with carriers, warehouses, and other stakeholders to coordinate the implementation of corrective actions to resolve issues and minimize delays
Being the Focal Point to provide timely and accurate status updates of all the Shipments in the "Trip Build"


Responsibilities


Ensure Quality of Tracking Data
Continuously track "Actuals" of all Track Events
Monitor "Milestones" of selected Track Events
Identify "Alerts" and resolve Shipment issues, such as delays, of selected "Milestones"
Serve as a liaison between TCT and other stakeholders to ensure clear communication and issue resolution
Leverage advanced Visibility technologies to enhance tracking and tracing capabilities
Escalate issues according to standard operating procedure


Qualifications


Engineering / logistics/ Business university or technical degree desirable
0-1 year experience in an industry working in transport area (Entry level)
Basic MSOffice Proficiency (Excel, Power Point)
English Speaker
Strong attention to detail and organizational skills
Proficiency in using tracking and TMS software
Ability to work under pressure and handle multiple tasks simultaneously
Communication and interpersonal skills to interact with diverse stakeholders
Ability to prioritize tasks, manage time effectively, and maintain organized records and reports
Urdu, Hindi or Nepali understanding could be helpful for the role

title: Quality Systems Support (DMS & TW)
description:
India, Hyderabad

Quality Systems Support (DMS & TW)Location: Hyderabad, India

Today, Lonza is a global leader in life sciences. We design and deliver solutions that enable scientists to accelerate innovation and improve health outcomes. We are seeking a Quality Systems Support (DMS & TW) to act as a key functional expert for the management, maintenance, and continuous improvement of the Documentum Management System (DMS) and TrackWise (TW) for the Hyderabad site.

Role Purpose / Summary

The Quality Systems Support ensures the accuracy, compliance, and readiness of quality documentation and records by providing operational administration, training, first-level support, and lifecycle stewardship. As a local administrator, the role collaborates with global system owners and local stakeholders to guarantee proper access control, data integrity, and alignment with global and local requirements, enabling efficient documentation management and system performance.

Key Accountabilities


DMS System Administration: Serve as the QM1 function/administrator for the site, maintaining master data and representing the site in global system governance and discussions.
User Support & Training: Provide first-level hotline support for site users and develop training materials to ensure compliant system operation.
Access Management: Assign permissions according to global and local user concepts, ensuring proper access control and segregation of duties for both DMS and TrackWise.
Documentation Lifecycle Management: Oversee the complete lifecycle of documents, including controlled copies, validity checks, periodic reviews, and updating related SOPs.
TrackWise Administration: Serve as the local administrator, maintaining master data and managing EiCR global permissions to ensure workflow alignment.
Communication & Resources: Maintain the DMS and TrackWise homepages on Lonza’s Intranet to ensure accurate communication of resources and guidance.
System Troubleshooting: Offer guidance and troubleshooting for documentation workflows, permissions, and general system usage issues.


Key Requirements

Domain Expertise


High School Diploma required; degree in Computer Science or Technology (or equivalent professional experience) preferred.
0–4 years of experience (Entry Level) in quality systems administration (DMS, TrackWise, or similar) in a regulated environment.
Knowledge of GMP and documentation requirements is beneficial.
Business fluent in English (required) and German (preferred).


Skills & Attributes


Technical Proficiency: Strong ability to translate technical and organizational content with precision and consistency.
Operational Excellence: Familiarity with terminology management, translation tools, and structured documentation with strong attention to detail.
Project & Time Management: Ability to manage multiple priorities in parallel and work effectively under tight timelines.
Customer Orientation: Effective communication and collaboration skills with a strong customer-oriented mindset.
Core Behaviors: Agility to challenge the status quo, business acumen to support company strategy, and a collaborative approach to sharing knowledge across teams.


About Lonza

At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. With more than 30 sites across five continents, our globally connected teams work together every day to manufacture the medicines of tomorrow. Our core values—Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion, and Integrity—reflect who we are and how we work together. Everyone’s ideas, big or small, have the potential to improve millions of lives. Innovation thrives when people from all backgrounds bring their unique perspectives to the table. Lonza is committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.

title: Pre-Sales Engineer
description:
Date: Mar 27, 2026

Location:

Hyderabad, TG, IN, 500032

Functional Area: Hybrid

Syntax is a leading Managed Cloud Provider for Mission Critical Enterprise Applications and has been providing comprehensive technology solutions to businesses of all sizes since 1972. Syntax has undisputed strength to implement and manage ERP deployments (Oracle, SAP) in a secure and resilient private, public or hybrid cloud. With strong technical and functional consulting services, and world-class monitoring and automation, Syntax serves some of North America’s largest corporations across a diverse range of industries. Syntax has offices worldwide, and partners with Oracle, SAP, AWS, Microsoft, IBM and other global technology leaders.

Job Title: Entry-Level Pre-Sales Engineer

Location: Hybrid/Remote In-Office

Job Type: Full-Time | Entry-Level

About The Role

We are seeking a highly motivated Entry-Level Pre-Sales Engineer to join our growing team. This role is designed for recent graduates eager to apply their technical and analytical skills in a customer-facing environment. The Pre-Sales Engineer will support Pre-Sales Solution Architects by conducting technology research, assisting with services and cloud pricing, and performing configuration item and contract reconciliation. This position offers the unique opportunity to be mentored by the most senior technical resources in the company, providing a solid foundation for career growth in pre-sales engineering, solution architecture, and enterprise IT consulting.

Key Responsibilities


Conduct technology research to support solution design and customer requirements.
Assist in pricing and configuration of IT services and cloud solutions by analyzing vendor pricing models and cost structures.
Support cloud pricing analysis and configuration for customer proposals.
Perform configuration item and contract reconciliation to ensure accuracy in customer agreements and solution designs.
Collaborate with Solution Architects to build technology solutions that align with customer needs.
Develop a strong understanding of enterprise computing concepts, including ERP, databases, networking, operating systems, data centers, and cloud computing.
Prepare and maintain documentation related to solution designs, pricing, and configurations.
Work with cross-functional teams, including sales, operations, and finance, to ensure accurate and competitive solutions.
Participate in training and mentorship programs led by senior engineers and solution architects.


Required Qualifications & Skills


Bachelor’s degree in Management Information Systems (MIS), Computer Science, Accounting, Finance, or a related business function with relevant computer science coursework.
Strong analytical and research skills with the ability to interpret and present technical information.
Basic knowledge of enterprise IT environments, including ERP systems, networking, operating systems, databases, data centers, and cloud computing platforms (AWS, Azure, Google Cloud).
Strong proficiency in Microsoft Excel and other financial or pricing analysis tools.
Ability to learn quickly and adapt to new technologies and business models.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a team-oriented environment.
No prior work experience required; internships or academic projects related to enterprise IT, cloud computing, or technology research are a plus.


What We Offer


Hands-on mentorship from senior technical leaders.
Exposure to cutting-edge enterprise technologies and cloud solutions.
A structured career development path in pre-sales engineering and solution architecture.
A dynamic and supportive work environment with opportunities to engage in real-world IT solution development.
Competitive salary and benefits package.


If you are a recent graduate with a passion for technology, enterprise IT, and business solutions, we encourage you to apply and start your journey with us!

Why Syntax?

Become a part of our success story and work in a company with exciting innovation projects that are causing a stir across the industry. We recently launched one of the world's most advanced manufacturing facilities based on SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP Digital Manufacturing Cloud for Execution - for Smart Press Shop, a pioneering joint venture between Porsche and forming specialist Schuler.


Competitive, above-average compensation
Global tourist: With us, you can also work from abroad from time to time
Flexible working time models, home office
Attractive benefits, e.g. company pension scheme or various health offers
A modern environment in which the "you" is part of it
Open feedback culture, flat hierarchies and a motivated team
Individual career planning with continuous training and coaching on the job


You see a personal challenge in this responsible task? Apply now - and become part of the SYNTAX team!
Show more

title: Intern
description:
TechnipFMC is committed to driving real change in the energy industry. Our ambition is to build a sustainable future through relentless innovation and global collaboration – and we want you to be part of it. You’ll be joining a culture that values curiosity, expertise, and ideas as well as equal opportunities, inclusion, and authenticity. Bring your unique energy to our team of more than 21,000 people worldwide, and discover a rewarding, fulfilling, and varied career that you can take in anywhere you want to go.

Job Purpose

We are looking for motivated and detail-oriented fresh diploma holders in Mechanical Engineering to join our manufacturing assembly team. This entry-level role offers hands-on training and exposure to the assembly of critical components used in oil and gas operations. Ideal candidates will have a strong foundation in mechanical principles and the ability to communicate effectively in English.

Job Description

Key Responsibilities


Assemble mechanical and electrical components as per engineering drawings and standard operating procedures
Operace hand tools, power tools, and basic machinery safely and efficiently Perform visual inspections and basic quality checks to ensure product accuracy and reliability
Collaborate with team members and supervisors to meet production goals
Maintain a clean and organized work environment
Adhere to safety standards and report any incidents or hazards promptly
Record work activities and maintain accurate production documentation


Qualifications

You are meant for this job if:


Diploma in Mechanical Engineering from a recognized institution
Fresh graduates are welcome: no prior experience required
Ability to read and interpret technical drawings and assembly instructions
Proficiency in reading, writing, and verbal communication in English is mandatory
Basic understanding of mechanical systems and manufacturing processes
Strong attention to detail and willingness. to learn
Physically fit and capable of working in a factory setting


Skills

Manufacturing Processes: Metals / Forging / Casting

Communication

Written and Verbal Communication



Being a global leader in the energy industry requires an inclusive and diverse environment. TechnipFMC promotes diversity, equity, and inclusion by ensuring equal opportunities to all ages, races, ethnicities, religions, sexual orientations, gender expressions, disabilities, or all other pluralities. We celebrate who you are and what you bring. Every voice matters and we encourage you to add to our culture.




TechnipFMC respects the rights and dignity of those it works with and promotes adherence to internationally recognized human rights principles for those in its value chain.

title: Assistant Manager - Growth & Development (DLHP-MST-New Hire Learning) - Hyderabad
description:
Summary

Position Summary

Assistant Manager, Deloitte Leadership & Human Potential (DLHP)

Portfolio: New Hire Experience in Milestones, Social Impact & Transitions team (MST) based in Hyderabad

Learning and Development (L&D) at Deloitte aims to bring out the best of every person and the best of every team, so we can deliver the best of Deloitte to our clients. Our focus includes:


Bringing out the leader in every person
A holistic approach to development that emphasizes not just education, but also self-awareness, exposure to real-world problems, and connections to people
Positioning our people to help tackle some of the most complex issues in business, our communities and society at large.


The team

Deloitte Leadership and Human Potential (L&HP) is focused on delivering a leading-edge, branded learning experience across the firm, emphasizing human capabilities as complementary to technical ones and setting the standard for creating growth-oriented and inclusive professionals and leaders for the future of work. DLHP will include focus areas ranging from the humanities – integrating purpose; diversity, equity and inclusion; well-being; Enduring Human Capabilities (curiosity, informed agility, resilience, connected teaming, divergent thinking, and social and emotional intelligence)—to coaching and leading people and teams, communication skills, growth and sales, industry and market skills, and client leadership.

Work you’ll do

Supporting New Hire Experience space within the Milestones, Social Impact & Transition (MST),your primary focus is building and executing new hire onboarding development strategies, initiatives, and programs for new hires in the firm. In DLHP, your primary focus is to manage and develop curriculum development activities for new hire learning portfolio – leveraging SMRs and existing materials, and reviewing deliverables for accuracy and quality.

Strategy Development

10%

 Plan & execute a world class New Hire Onboarding experience strategy in USI while staying in constant touch with US team counterparts to maintain US-USI alignment

 Plan and lead curriculum development activities; leverage SMRs and existing materials; review deliverables for accuracy and quality.

 Conduct external research to find impactful leading-edge capability-building strategies, partnerships, and programs to support strategy implementation in the New Hire Learning & New Hire Onboarding space

Stakeholder Relationship Management

15%

 Build effective relationships with key stakeholders related to development projects. These range from Staff, Senior Staff, Managers, Senior Managers, SVPs, PPMDs, other USI LFHP team members, L&D SPOCs of other businesses, external empaneled vendor partners, US team members etc.

 Work closely with multiple teams like Talent Services, Office Experience, Regional Managing Principal’s office, Food & Beverage, Security, Operations etc. across the seven office locations to ensure smooth onboarding experiences.

 Participate in or lead project teams to build positive, collaborative partnerships among business stakeholders, and peers. Prepare draft presentations and reports to support stakeholder meetings.

 Maintain relationships with content-based leads (SMRs).

Operations Management

35%

 Identify areas for operational improvement, making suggestions and driving change to gain efficiencies and improve learning impact.

 Collaborate with Talent Services (Learning Development, Learning Operations, and Learning Delivery) to create and deliver leading-edge learning solutions.

 Execute assigned programs within budget and timeline.

 Run and analyze reports to provide preliminary insights on program success (Voice of Customer sentiment analysis).

 Manage all activities to be on-time and within budget, tracking against original plan to determine efficiency.

 Execute the CPE/certification process from objective mapping to form submission to participant receiving credits.

 Collaborate with Learning Operations to maintain accurate curriculum data integrity, maintain historical archives, and respond to ad hoc requests for curriculum information.

 Support cross-team operational initiatives as needed.

Curriculum Management

35%

 Work closely with the USI New Hire Experience (NHE) lead in carving out development and onboarding journeys for professionals in the region

 Collaborate with USI & US NHE team during weekly connects to highlight and address any India geo-specific content deviations and work towards achieving a consistent program design. Share content updates tailored to USI specific geography

 Consult with a USI NHE team to update content and modify the modalities of the DLaunch Program in response to organization-wide workforce transformation initiatives

 Own and execute end-to-end onboarding program experiences by understanding the brand these unique programs have built and be the brand ambassador of these programs by communicating the vision and purpose of these offerings

 Own and undertake quarterly / half-yearly Voice of Customer sentiment analysis by taking note of all feedback received during new hire onboarding across locations and suggesting location-specific Plan of Actions while keeping US NHE teams in sync

 Work hand-in-glove with NHE Lead to be the face of all New Hire Orientation programs in USI

 Appreciate the vision of MST team and then tailor onboarding learning experiences for new hires and develop learning offerings for NHE accordingly

 Support end-to-end participant journey, facilitator sourcing, and all related communications.

 Build on, and keep abreast of strong knowledge of development tools, modalities, and learning methodologies.

Resource Management & Development

5%

 Create a teaming environment which encourages sharing of best practices, driving innovation, and continuing improvement.

 Demonstrate positive team collaboration and inspire teaming culture that fosters individual and team growth—builds on strengths and showcases needle movement on developmental areas.

 Works closely with Talent Services Facilitators to identify gaps in resourcing for multiple DLaunch sessions across locations and provide inputs to solve for resourcing

 Complete and submit professional development and performance review documentation.

 Contribute towards an inclusive and high-performing teaming culture that maximizes team member contribution by creating an environment of mutual support and shared responsibility.

Qualifications

Required:


Bachelor’s Degree
At least 4-8 years of related professional experience
Collaborative mindset to share and implement best practices
Basic instructional design skills
Demonstrated project and program management experience: organization, managing details, keeping multiple tasks/projects on track
Ability to communicate effectively and credibly, both verbally and in writing, with audiences at all management levels
Ability to work autonomously and effectively in a fast-paced, complex, multi-tasking virtual environment
Excellent Microsoft Office skills
Up to 15% travel could be required based on role responsibilities


Preferred:


Experience working in a learning and development organization specifically on new hire onboarding programs
Prior consulting or professional services experience


Location: Hyderabad

How You’ll Grow

At Deloitte, our professional development plan focuses on helping people at every level of their career to identify and use their strengths to do their best work every day. From entry-level employees to senior leaders, we believe there’s always room to learn. We offer opportunities to help sharpen skills in addition to hands-on experience in the global, fast-changing business world.From on-the-job learning experiences to formal development programs, our professionals have a variety of opportunities to continue to grow throughout their career.

Benefits

At Deloitte, we know that great people make a great organization. We value our people and offer employees a broad range of benefits. Learn more about what working at Deloitte can mean for you.

Deloitte’s culture

Our positive and supportive culture encourages our people to do their best work every day. We celebrate individuals by recognizing their uniqueness and offering them the flexibility to make daily choices that can help them to be healthy, centered, confident, and aware. We offer well-being programs and are continuously looking for new ways to maintain a culture where our people excel and lead healthy, happy lives. Learn more about Life at Deloitte.

Corporate citizenship

Deloitte is led by a purpose: to make an impact that matters. This purpose defines who we are and extends to relationships with our clients, our people and our communities.We believe that business has the power to inspire and transform. We focus on education, giving, skill-based volunteerism, and leadership to help drive positive social impact in our communities. Learn more about Deloitte’s impact on the world.

Recruiter tips

We want job seekers exploring opportunities at Deloitte to feel prepared and confident. To help you with your interview, we suggest that you do your research: know some background about the organization and the business area you’re applying to. Check out recruiting tips from Deloitte professionals.

#CA-BK1

Our purpose

Deloitte’s purpose is to make an impact that matters for our people, clients, and communities. At Deloitte, purpose is synonymous with how we work every day. It defines who we are. Our purpose comes through in our work with clients that enables impact and value in their organizations, as well as through our own investments, commitments, and actions across areas that help drive positive outcomes for our communities.

Our people and culture

Our inclusive culture empowers our people to be who they are, contribute their unique perspectives, and make a difference individually and collectively. It enables us to leverage different ways of thinking, ideas and perspectives, and bring more creativity and innovation to help solve our clients’ most complex challenges. This makes Deloitte one of the most rewarding places to work.

Professional development

At Deloitte, professionals have the opportunity to work with some of the best and discover what works best for them. Here, we prioritize professional growth, offering diverse learning and networking opportunities to help accelerate careers and enhance leadership skills. Our state-of-the-art DU: The Leadership Center in India, located in Hyderabad, represents a tangible symbol of our commitment to the holistic growth and development of our people. Explore DU: The Leadership Center in India .

Benefits To Help You Thrive

At Deloitte, we know that great people make a great organization. Our comprehensive rewards program helps us deliver a distinctly Deloitte experience that helps that empowers our professionals to thrive mentally, physically, and financially—and live their purpose. To support our professionals and their loved ones, we offer a broad range of benefits. Eligibility requirements may be based on role, tenure, type of employment and/ or other criteria. Learn more about what working at Deloitte can mean for you.

Recruiting tips

From developing a stand out resume to putting your best foot forward in the interview, we want you to feel prepared and confident as you explore opportunities at Deloitte. Check out recruiting tips from Deloitte recruiters.

title: Senior Associate - Strategy&
description:
Strategy& – Senior Associate – Strategy Consulting

Sectors – Consumer || FMCG || Industrial || FMEG || E-Commerce || Retail

Location – Mumbai || Gurgaon




Senior Associates at Strategy&, part of the PwC network, typically bring 3–5 years of experience and have demonstrated strong consulting or corporate strategy exposure across sectors and problem statements. They play a critical role in solving strategic challenges, working shoulder-to-shoulder with clients to address their most pressing issues — from growth and market entry to digital transformation and business model innovation.

Senior Associates are expected to contribute to the development of corporate and business unit strategies, evaluate market opportunities, structure problems, conduct analyses, and support end-to-end project delivery. Common areas of focus include Corporate Strategy, Growth Strategy, Go-to-Market Design, Market Entry, Digital Strategy and Transformation Projects.

Key Attributes and Abilities We Seek

As a Senior Associate, you will be expected to demonstrate analytical thinking, initiative, collaboration, and high personal drive. PwC Professional skills and responsibilities at this level include, but are not limited to:

Strong problem structuring and solving capabilities (quantitative and qualitative), and the ability to apply structured thinking across different industries and contexts.
Demonstrated ability to translate complex, ambiguous business issues into clear problem statements with actionable insights.
Conducting and synthesizing market research, primary interviews, competitive benchmarking, and data analysis to derive impactful recommendations.
Collaborating effectively in a fast-paced, team-oriented environment, providing and seeking regular feedback and clarification.
Supporting engagement leadership in project planning, workstream management, and client communication.
Drafting high-quality deliverables and client-ready presentations in a clear, structured, and insightful manner.
Engaging with client teams to understand business context, validate findings, and co-create solutions.
Driving internal initiatives and contributing to knowledge sharing and practice development.

What We Look For

We look for exceptional individuals who combine strong consulting fundamentals with intellectual curiosity, business acumen, and a collaborative mindset. Our ideal candidates demonstrate:

Strong academic and professional achievement; an MBA from a top-tier institution.
Experience in strategy, business planning, transformation, or business development roles in top-tier consulting firms or corporate strategy teams.
Strong analytical, communication, and storytelling skills, with the ability to distill insights from data and clearly articulate strategic narratives.
A reputation for problem-solving, creative thinking, and ability to connect the dots across business functions.
The ability to work with multiple stakeholders, handle ambiguity, and adapt in dynamic client situations.
A natural inclination toward collaboration and mentorship, with emerging leadership qualities.
High levels of ownership, self-motivation, and attention to detail.
Passion for a consulting career — being on the front line of solving real-world business problems.

What You’ll Bring (Experience & Qualifications)

3–5 years of experience in strategy consulting or relevant corporate roles.
1-3 years of experience in consumer, retail or industrials sector.
An MBA from a top tier institution.
Practical exposure in one or more of the following areas:
Corporate and Business Unit Strategy
Growth Strategy, Market Entry, Go-to-Market Design
Organizational or Digital Transformation
Business development, marketing, or sales strategy
Strong command of Excel, PowerPoint, and other analytical tools to structure and communicate insights.
Ability to adapt across industries and business issues, showing a solid understanding of market dynamics and enterprise levers.
Demonstrated experience in leading or co-leading workstreams and contributing to impactful client outcomes.




Sectors - Consumer || FMCG || Industrial || FMEG || E Commerce - Location - Mumbai || Gurgaon

Enabling Areas – Marketing, Brand, and Communications

Marketing, Brand, and Communications – Intern, Mumbai (3-6 months)

What impact will you make?

Every day, your work will make an impact that matters, while you thrive in a dynamic culture of inclusion, collaboration and high performance. As the undisputed leader in professional services, Deloitte is where you’ll find unrivalled opportunities to succeed and realise your full potential

Work you’ll do:

Within the Marketing, Brand, and Communications team, your work will be aligned with marketing and engagement initiatives. This entails ideation and execution of marketing campaigns for change and business initiatives across various communication channels.




Essentials:

Support digital marketing and campaign planning initiatives while contributing to storytelling and marketing communication efforts
Being proactive with fresh ideas and taking ownership to execute campaigns and marketing strategy
Staying updated with the latest innovations and trends in marketing and social media communications
Comfort with producing short format format-friendly and engaging content from end-to-end
Strong language skills, both verbal and written
Strong skills in PowerPoint, Canva and any other creative tools to be able to support on visualising and creating periodic progress reports for the team




Qualifications:

A graduate degree in marketing, mass media, or similar fields is an advantage, as are additional certifications in digital marketing-related programs. However, we are open to anyone with a creative bent of mind.
Should have completed bachelors at least.
Currently in Mumbai and will to travel to Thane




Experience:

Prior experience in digital marketing, branding, social media marketing or similar is mandatory.




Your role as a leader

At Deloitte India, we believe in the importance of leadership at all levels. We expect our people to embrace and live our purpose by challenging themselves to identify issues that are most important for our clients, our people, and for society and make an impact that matters.




In addition to living our purpose, Executive across our organization:

Builds own understanding of our purpose and values; explores opportunities for impact
Demonstrates strong commitment to personal learning and development; acts as a brand
ambassador to help attract top talent
Understands expectations and demonstrates personal accountability for keeping performance on track
Actively focuses on developing effective communication and relationship-building skills
Understands how their daily work contributes to the priorities of the team and the business




How you’ll grow

At Deloitte, our professional development plan focuses on helping people at every level of their career to identify and use their strengths to do their best work every day. From entry-level employees to senior leaders, we believe there’s always room to learn. We offer opportunities to help build world-class skills in addition to hands-on experience in the global, fast-changing business world. From on-the-job learning experiences to formal development programs at Deloitte University, our professionals have a variety of opportunities to continue to grow throughout their career.




Our purpose

Deloitte is led by a purpose: To make an impact that matters.

Every day, Deloitte people are making a real impact in the places they live and work. We pride ourselves on doing not only what is good for clients, but also what is good for our people and the communities in which we live and work—always striving to be an organization that is held up as a role model of quality, integrity, and positive change.

title: Trainee Engineer, NOC
description:
hackajob is collaborating with Scale Computing to connect them with exceptional professionals for this role.

Who We Are

Acumera is now Scale Computing! Scale Computing is a global leader in edge computing, hyperconverged infrastructure, and managed networking solutions. We deliver innovative, secure, andscalable technology that powers critical operations worldwide.

About The Role

Trainee/Associate Engineer will oversee daily operation of support desk functions that includes taking calls as first level support, entering the reported problems in the ticketing system, solving user problems, proper escalation of open problems and dealing with the customers. Will also participate in preventative maintenance, troubleshooting, configuring and providing technical

support for end-users.

Great Candidates Will Look Like This

A team player who can work well within the team and collaborate cross-functionally, especially in a remote environment.

An excellent communicator who is efficient and effective with customers and colleagues via instant messages, email, video conferencing and in-person interactions.Enjoy troubleshooting and problem-solving analytically.

Essential duties and responsibilities include the following: (Other duties may be assigned.)

Provide helpful and professional assistance to customers via phone, email and tickets.

Create or update support tickets as required for phone calls.

Document all calls, actions, and any relevant information in ticket notes.

Handle tickets created by customers via email.

Follow up on existing tickets proactively and within specified time frames.

Resolve or escalate tickets in a timely manner, within SLA guidelines.

Interact with other departments and teams, collaborating on tickets or solutions.

Evaluate customers’ needs and determine the appropriate action.

Troubleshoot complex technical issues with customers via phone.

Understand and follow security and other guidelines for customers’ accounts.

Process voicemails, creating or updating support tickets as necessary.

Write and send customized email responses to customers for specific situations.

Understand and adhere to Scale Computing policies and procedures.

Place outbound calls to resolve internally-detected issues.

Be professional and courteous at all times, especially when speaking or conversing with customers.

Technical Requirements

Knowledge of at least some of the following:

General computer systems

PCs, laptops, mobile devices.

Current and recent Operating Systems

Windows, Mac OS, Linux

Routing Protocols and concepts

Static and Dynamic routing, Distance, Priority, routing table, Default Gateway

Switching Protocols and concepts

VLAN, Trunks and link bonding, Spanning Tree and RSTP, MAC Address table,ARP.

Networking Protocols and concepts

TCP, UDP, DHCP, DNS, VPN, ICMP, TELNET, SSH FTP, HTTP(s), IPSEC, SSL,etc.

Network troubleshooting tools such as PING, TRACEROUTE, NSLOOKUP, IPCONFIG, etc.

OSI Model – Advanced understanding of layers 1-4

Subnetting and Supernetting

Firewall rules and policies, Access Lists

Deep understanding of technical concepts within networking environments as well as security, risk and compliance.

Entry Level knowledge of Switches, Routers, Firewalls, and Networking in general is required. Must be detail-oriented and must be able to work well in a team environment.

Reporting Structure

Reports to Tier 1 Manager - NOC.

Education And Experience

Bachelor’s degree (B.E./B.Tech/BCA) in a technical domain such as Computer Science, Telecommunication, Electronics & Communication, Information Technology, or a related field.

Minimum experience in the networking domain is preferred. (0-2 years)

Experience Working In Helpdesk Or Technical Support Is Preferred.

Industry certifications like CompTIA Network+, CCNA, CCNP, JNCIA, Wireshark, etc. are preferred.

Perks of Acumera

Group medical insurance.

Paid company holidays.

Casual dress code

Cab facilities for employees working in shifts.

Fully stocked snacks at office.

Vibrant and Inclusive Workplace Atmosphere.

Flexible work environment and an opportunity to grow as we grow.

Scale Computing is an equal-opportunity employer. All final candidates will be subject to a pre-employment background check.

title: Investment Banking Analyst
description:
Investment Banking Analyst (Graduates and Experienced Hires) - Mumbai







Our Client

Our subsidiary CF Invest, is a dedicated recruitment firm focused on positions in the investment sector.




We are recruiting on behalf of our client, one of the bulge bracket bank and pre-eminent investment banks.




This tier one firm works with us to select the highest calibre talent in the global markets at entry and experienced entry level across their global offices on an ongoing basis through the first half of each year, with the view of onboarding immediately or upon graduation.




Our client is looking for two professionals to join their Investment Banking team in Mumbai as an Analyst and use their skills and ideas to make an impact on the success of the bank. You will either be placed in their FIG, TMT or Energy Group or possibly another division as they also seek cross industry including but not limited to Industrials, Healthcare and Consumer.




The role requires talent to advise clients on various financial transactions, including mergers and acquisitions, equity/equity-linked and debt financings, joint ventures, restructurings, and financial investments.







Your Focus

As an Investment Banking Analyst, your focus is on supporting clients through complex capital markets and advisory transactions, including mergers, acquisitions, and capital raising. You will work closely with senior bankers to analyse opportunities, structure transactions, and help clients make informed strategic decisions.




The role is execution-driven and detail-intensive. You will build and maintain financial analyses, support transaction execution, and contribute to client materials used in live deals. You will also help assess risk, ensure adherence to regulatory and compliance requirements, and maintain the highest standards of accuracy, judgement, and professionalism.




This role suits someone who is analytically rigorous, commercially aware, and motivated by working at pace on high-stakes transactions. You will be expected to take ownership of your work, communicate clearly with colleagues and clients, and contribute meaningfully to deal delivery in a demanding, regulated environment.







Responsibilities

Support the execution of investment banking transactions, including M&A, capital raising, and strategic advisory mandates
Build and maintain detailed financial models and analyses to evaluate transactions and strategic options
Prepare client-facing materials, including pitch decks, information memoranda, and transaction documentation
Conduct company, industry, and market research to support deal origination and execution
Assist senior bankers in coordinating transaction workstreams and managing timelines
Support due diligence processes, including data analysis and coordination with internal and external stakeholders
Contribute to client development activities through research, analysis, and preparation of marketing materials
Assess transaction-related risks and support adherence to regulatory, compliance, and internal control requirements
Maintain high standards of accuracy, organisation, and confidentiality across all work
Work effectively with colleagues across banking, markets, legal, risk, and compliance teams




This role is about supporting the execution of complex investment banking transactions and helping clients make informed strategic and capital decisions.




You can absorb detailed financial and commercial information quickly, analyse companies, transactions, and market conditions with rigour, and form clear, well-reasoned conclusions. You approach problems with structure and discipline, and you are comfortable working under pressure as deals progress and priorities evolve.







Minimum Qualifications




Graduate – Analyst

Bachelor’s degree ideally in Finance, Economics, Accounting, Business, or a related discipline. We accept also individuals from any degree, especially those numerate based
Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to produce clear client-ready materials
High attention to detail and strong organisational skills
Ability to work effectively under pressure and manage multiple priorities
Sound judgement and a strong understanding of risk, compliance, and regulatory expectations
We consider any degree or subject and although your grade indicates your ability to absorb data and apply it, your degree grade does not influence your chance of applying to us and being interviewed.







Process

This application shall be open until we reach a cap of applicants, so please apply early. We are hiring for our client across their key global markets.




Be sure to Follow Us on LinkedIn for updates on this vacancy and others: https://www.linkedin.com/company/cfcorp




Your first step is to apply with your LinkedIn profile and CV. We do not need a covering letter. Due to the volume of applications we receive, we are unfortunately unable to provide individual feedback.







Reward Structure

Analyst annual base salary of 125,000 and up to 150,000 United States Dollars depending on experience. Your salary will be paid in Indian Rupees and bonus in USD
Bonus program of on-target bonus of 50% of base salary and up to 125% for high-achievers
Relocation is provided by the company.

title: Data Quality Analyst
description:
We invite you to come grow with us.

Inizio Ignite is the global advisory partner for health and life sciences, encompassing connected expertise across Research Partnership, Putnam, Vynamic, and STEM. Guided by purpose, passion, and precision, we collaborate with clients to ignite impact, accelerate performance, and deliver lasting change for patients. By uniting strategy, insights, and innovation with a unique depth and breadth of expertise, we drive transformation and shape the future of health.

Our Research Partnership healthcare market research team provides research and insights to inform and power healthcare decisions. Serving a variety of clients across the lifecycle, our expertise includes data, research & insights (proprietary syndicated and custom research) and brand tracking (ATU). Proudly part of Inizio – the market-leading commercialization partner for health and life sciences. From early-stage development to loss of exclusivity, we simplify complexity, unlock value, and make collaboration seamless at every pivotal moment – supporting confident, insight-driven decisions that move innovation forward.

About the role:

The Data Quality Analyst role will work alongside colleagues in Europe, India and Singapore on major Brand Equity tracking projects for a global client covering multiple Therapy Areas across a number of international markets. The position will report into the Program Data Manager, based in the UK, with direct colleagues in analytics based in India and the UK. This is a high profile client and project for our business, providing a platform for the project team to have a significant impact on the client’s brand strategies.

The role will involve working with our Research project teams, the Data Management & Dashboards team and with analytics experts to investigate and check data across multiple workstreams for our major tracking programs, primarily using Python.

About the team:

The Gurugram hub office is part of our global team, partnering with colleagues across different functional areas and specialisms. This is an exciting period of growth for Inizio Ignite | Research Partnership, as we expand into the fast-growing Indian market.

Your primary role:

As Data Quality Analyst, you will support the Program Data Manager to ensure data quality and consistency throughout the reporting cycle on our Major Tracking programs. You will also support with advising the Research project teams and Data Management & Dashboard teams based in Europe, India and Singapore.

Technical Expertise


Python (3 to 6 years) experience for data processing, automation, and pipeline support
Experience managing end-to-end data pipelines (ingestion, transformation, storage, outputs)


Database Expertise


Strong SQL skills using PostgreSQL, MySQL, or MS SQL
Experience with data modelling and working with data marts / warehouses to support dashboard performance.


Dashboarding & Visualization


Hands-on experience with Power BI, Tableau, or similar BI tools
Ability to support dashboard population, maintenance, and data validation


Cloud & Platform Exposure (Nice to Have)


Exposure to cloud-based data processing environments (AWS or Google Cloud)
Familiarity with version control and structured deployment processes


Soft Skills & Behaviors


Clear communicator with both technical and non-technical stakeholders
Strong ownership and accountability for end-to-end data delivery
High attention to detail with a strong data quality mindset
Ability to prioritise and manage multiple tasks in a deadline-driven environment
Strong analytical, problem-solving skills and Comfortable working in global, cross-functional teams


Life at Inizio Ignite | Research Partnership:

We love what we do and continuously strive for innovation and creativity. We believe everyone should continuously be learning and provide:


A comprehensive training and development programme for all staff, from entry level apprentice to senior management
Opportunities for international travel and collaboration
A relaxed and friendly working environment


Further information:

This is a full-time role and is available in a hybrid pattern, based at our fantastic Inizio Advisory location in Gurugram. This role’s working hours will be 13:30 to 22:30 from Monday to Thursday and 11:30 to 20:30 on Friday.

Any specific information that you would want us to gather from the candidates.

Technical:


Experience with Python
Experience with PostgreSQL, MySQL, or MS SQL
Experience managing end-to-end data pipelines
Experience with data warehousing / data marts


Capabilities:


Ability to work autonomously with limited direct oversight / instruction
Client-facing capabilities to explain data management challenges/requirements
Working with a globally distributed team


Don't meet every job requirement? That's okay! Our company is dedicated to building a diverse, inclusive, and authentic workplace. If you're excited about this role, but your experience doesn't perfectly fit every qualification, we encourage you to apply anyway. You may be just the right person for this role or others.

title: Supervisor/Executive F&A
description:
Responsible for managing key financial operations including revenue recognition, project accounting, working capital monitoring, statutory compliances, audit support, MIS reporting, and financial controls. The role ensures accuracy, transparency, and timeliness of financial data essential for management decision?making and statutory obligations.

Key Responsibilities1. Revenue & Cost Accounting


Preparation and review of RSV (Revenue) statements, RSV entry reversals, and RSV interest workings.
Monitoring of Project Valuation Reports, performance variance, and completed project cost provisions.
Analysis of OI/OB sales, PLR (Price Level Reserve) provision, and DLP (Defect Liability Period) provision.
Ensuring accurate project cost capitalization including AUC details and CWIP statement.


2. Working Capital & Operational Finance


Preparation of Working Capital reports and monitoring of receivables/payables.
Tracking project-wise unadjusted advances and reconciliation with the project team.
Verification of Cash Flow workings and supporting treasury functions.
Compilation of Yard Details and inventory?related financial impact.


3. MIS, Reporting & Management Presentations


Development of Labour Dashboard, Fixed Cost Report, Capacity Utilization details, and other MIS reports for management review.
Preparing Audit Committee data, management decks, and Deloitte PPT support.
Analysis of Forex Exposure and reporting to management.


4. Compliance, Audit & Controls


Supporting statutory, internal, and external audits including ICFR documentation, RPT (Related Party Transaction) reporting, and balance confirmations.
Ensuring Compliance of Wages through ECS and other labour/finance?related statutory requirements.
Preparation of ECL (Expected Credit Loss) entries as per financial standards.
Monitoring Asset Disposal accounting and documentation.


5. Special Projects & Cross?Functional Coordination


Maintenance and review of Master Project financial data.
Tracking Orders Lost & Orders Deferred and coordinating with sales/commercial teams.
Contributing to CLCS reporting, PAT built?up, and other strategic financial analyses.
Coordination with departments for validating data for financial controls and reporting.


Core Competencies


Strong financial analysis and accounting knowledge
Proficiency in project accounting
Excellent MIS preparation & presentation skills
Understanding of audit & compliance frameworks
Ability to work cross?functionally with project, operations, and commercial teams
Attention to detail and strong reconciliation skills

Jobs may range from highly supervised entry-level workers, technicians, and administrative staff to experienced individual contributors with robust technical or practical knowledge in specific areas, who have an ability to execute specialized work to support operations. At the top of this career stream, individuals work independently and apply standards, yet can also make departures from established processes to resolve problems. A colleague at this level works on tasks of limited scope and complexity, exercises independent judgment within defined boundaries and guidelines and typically under supervision. Colleagues have knowledge of standardized work routines and methods, general facts and information but may need to escalate non-routine problems to a more experienced colleague or supervisor. title: ERP Product Designer (Fresher / Entry-Level)
description:
Job Description

About the Role

We are looking for a highly driven and accountable Product Designer who can take ownership of the complete ERP design lifecycle.

This role is ideal for freshers or early-career candidates who have strong communication skills, logical thinking, and the ability to understand business processes and convert them into structured product workflows.

Key Responsibilities

Client Interaction & Demo


Conduct product demos and explain ERP capabilities to clients
Understand client business processes and requirements


Requirement Gathering & BRD


Create detailed Business Requirement Documents (BRD)
Ensure requirements are clearly documented and approved


Workflow Design


Create structured workflows and process flows
Compare existing systems with ERP solutions and identify gaps
Get client approval before moving to design stage


Wireframing


Design wireframes using Figma after workflow confirmation
Ensure alignment between workflows and UI


Client Communication


Explain workflows, system logic, and development process
Handle feedback and refine outputs


Development Coordination


Work closely with developers to ensure correct implementation
Provide clarity and ensure alignment with requirements


Testing & Delivery


Coordinate with testers to ensure a bug-free product
Participate in final delivery and client approval


What We’re Looking For


Strong communication and presentation skills
Good analytical and problem-solving ability
Ability to understand business workflows quickly
Basic knowledge of Figma (or willingness to learn fast)
High ownership, accountability, and responsibility mindset
Confidence to interact directly with clients


Selection Process (Important)

We follow a practical evaluation approach instead of traditional interviews:

5-Day Real-Time Assessment


Candidates will work on live, practical tasks simulating real project scenarios
Tasks will evaluate:
Requirement understanding
Communication
Workflow thinking
Problem-solving
Ownership & accountability

Final selection will be based on performance during these 5 days

Who Should Apply


Freshers ready to take real responsibility from Day 1
Candidates who prefer hands-on learning over theoretical interviews
Individuals confident in communication and structured thinking


Why Join GwayERP


Direct exposure to real client interactions
Opportunity to work on end-to-end product lifecycle
Fast learning environment with high ownership roles
Build strong foundation in ERP product design & business systems
